narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing no must feel like a door slamming shut, especially after all your hard work. Our model looked at your profile and predicted a 41% chance of not passing the bar exam on your first try, which tipped the scales against admission because we have to prioritize those with the clearest paths to success. The biggest factor pulling toward that prediction was your Year 3 decile of 2, meaning you ranked in the bottom 20% of your peers that year, painting a picture of challenges sustaining strong performance over time. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 27.5, which falls short of the benchmarks that usually signal bar exam readiness. Your Year 1 decile of 2 reinforced this, showing that early struggles set a pattern the model couldn't ignore. Even with your upper-middle family income, which might offer some support, it didn't outweigh those academic hurdles in the model's view—in fact, it nudged slightly toward risk based on patterns we've seen. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.8 added to the concern, as it suggests foundational gaps that could trip you up later. Being a full-time Black female student is something we celebrate for the diversity you bring, yet the model saw subtle patterns where similar profiles faced higher hurdles on the bar. Together, these pieces formed a story of resilience needed but steep odds ahead, leading to this tough call. That said, this isn't the end—many with profiles like yours have boosted their paths by targeting weak spots like test prep or GPA-building grad work. You've got the drive; now channel it into proving the model wrong. We're rooting for you.
